preview_text:
The model favors France at 65%, with Senegal assigned a 10% chance of victory. This represents a substantial gap in win probability, though not an elimination-round disparity. Senegal's underdog status reflects a meaningful but surmountable deficit in the forecast.

The W/D/L spread shows France as the clear lean, yet the draw sits at 25%—a material outcome that signals competitive balance in underlying match dynamics. A one-goal margin or a deadlock through 90 minutes carries real probability mass, indicating this is not a heavily lopsided contest despite France's favorites' label.

Model and market are closely aligned across all three outcomes, differing by 4 percentage points or fewer on each line. The sportsbook consensus sits marginally lower on France (64%) and higher on Senegal (14%) relative to the model, but these gaps fall within typical variance. Polymarket is similarly tight with the statistical forecast. No meaningful divergence exists between sources.
